meta_title: For Founders: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Solving Founders' Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no project ideas. Learn to leverage Claude Code skills and Antigravity to build, validate, and launch your next SaaS MVP at speed.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap stems from lack of technical execution speed. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you transform abstract pain points into rapid, production-ready prototypes. This workflow bridges the gap between ideation and revenue, allowing you to build, test, and pivot SaaS products in mere hours.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a graveyard for ambition. You have the drive to build a SaaS, but the paralysis of 'what to build'—or worse, the fear of the technical lift—keeps your revenue at zero. You aren't suffering from a lack of creativity; you are suffering from a lack of a high-velocity feedback loop.

True founders don't wait for lightning-bolt ideas. They build, iterate, and refine until a market need emerges. By mastering Claude Code and the Antigravity framework, you turn your terminal into an engine of continuous product discovery. It is time to stop thinking about your next project and start shipping it.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Use Antigravity to decompose high-level market pain points into structured technical requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the top 5 pain points for remote project managers and propose a micro-SaaS solution that solves one specific bottleneck. Output the core features as a functional requirements document.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review the requirements.md file and create a project initialization plan.'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Generate the directory structure and foundational files to ensure a lean, scalable codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, modular Next.js 14 architecture with Tailwind CSS, focusing on a single-page MVP flow. Include a clear folder structure for components, services, and API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate project structure and install core dependencies for a Next.js MVP.'

steps.title: Implement Core Business Logic
steps.description: Leverage Claude Code to write the primary functional logic while maintaining strict type safety.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for the user authentication and data processing service. Use clean code principles and ensure all edge cases are handled in the controller.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Implement the core business logic in src/services/engine.ts based on the requirements.md.'

steps.title: Automated Testing and Validation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate unit tests that validate your business logic against the defined requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive test suite using Jest and React Testing Library for the core service. Ensure 90% coverage for the happy path and common error states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate and execute test suite for the core business logic.'

steps.title: Deployment and Feedback Loop
steps.description: Prepare the build for deployment to a cloud provider to start gathering real-world user feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment configuration script for Vercel/AWS. Include environmental variable management and a CI/CD pipeline definition.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Configure the project for production deployment and verify all build settings.'

pro_tips: Treat your 'requirements.md' as your single source of truth; Claude Code performs best when context is centralized and updated.
Use Antigravity to draft your database schema before writing a single line of code to prevent costly refactors later.
Always run Claude Code with the '--verbose' flag during the initial build phase to spot hallucinated dependencies early.
Implement feature flags immediately so you can ship 'broken' features to a subset of users for rapid validation.
When stuck, use the 'claude ask' command to perform a self-code review of your current branch to identify technical debt before it compounds.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to a staging environment, share the link with 10 target users, and use the feedback to refine your 'requirements.md' for the next iteration cycle.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting 'no project ideas' be the excuse that keeps you from your first $1k MRR. With the right tooling, your output speed is your competitive advantage. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
